cue: a for-all-n relation from a running partial sum
note: subtract index-shifted copies; the shared block cancels to a per-index relation

cue: a for-all-n relation from a sliding-window product
note: divide index-shifted copies; shared factors cancel to a multiplicative recurrence

cue: a selection under a spacing constraint, each pick forcing an exclusion
note: amortize each included pick against a forced skip to bound the selection size

cue: an optimizer admitting a one-step-perturbed admissible candidate
note: read the governing inequality off the admissible perturbation

cue: an inequality comparing an exponential r^t against a linear quantity
note: a tangent-line / Bernoulli bound linearizes the exponential contribution

cue: coinciding upper and lower bounds on a quantity
note: coinciding bounds force equality throughout the chain

cue: k terms each at least m with total km
note: the tight total forces every term equal to m

cue: a product of positive reals with fixed sum
note: split the fixed sum into equal/complementary parts to extremize the product

cue: a sum multiplied by its reciprocal-sum
note: expanding the cross terms regroups them into controlled diagonal products

cue: a target represented with a prescribed number of distinct summands
note: pad to the count with value-canceling sign-symmetric pairs

cue: a prescribed multiset of pairwise differences realized by points on a line
note: place points as partial sums of powers of a tailored root so block-sums collapse

cue: a self-similar multiset built from two half-size copies
note: recursive split T_m = (T_{m-1}+1) union {1..m}, two copies concatenated

cue: more collected items than a finite state set admits
note: more items than available states forces a state to repeat

cue: two divisibility relations sharing a common left factor
note: subtract one from a multiple of the other to collapse onto a tighter divisibility

cue: a prime power equal to a product of two positive factors
note: each factor is forced to be a pure power of that prime

cue: a finite integer set where each pairwise difference divides both endpoints
note: a fixed scaled gadget gives an infinite family

cue: a fixed stencil move applied uniformly on a board, reachability asked
note: annihilate the stencil with a position-weighting/coloring for a conserved quantity

cue: a process whose bounded monotone count targets its own extreme value
note: classify which moves keep it fixed, forcing it to reach the target or halt

cue: a merge fusing two equal piles into one double pile
note: doubling-tournament ladder plus a reservoir pile

cue: a process closed under addition and coordinatewise maximum, reaching a target tuple
note: build spike/basis vectors from generators spanning the needed span

cue: a prescribed per-position shift realized by transposing complementary-length blocks
note: pieces sized n, n-1, ... paired with 1, 2, ... each summing to n; swap each pair

cue: a point on a side's extension beyond a vertex, supplementing an apex angle
note: the supplement manufactures the second equal-angle pair for AA similarity

cue: two triangles with parallel corresponding sides
note: parallel correspondence forces a homothety/translation; locate its center

cue: two triangles sharing both circumcentre and orthocentre
note: they share a centroid and nine-point circle (-1/2 homothety)

cue: the second intersection point of two circles through a shared vertex
note: that point is the spiral-similarity center carrying one chord to the other

cue: two triangles sharing a vertex, both similar to a common reference
note: they are directly similar via a spiral similarity centered at the shared vertex

cue: a circle point equidistant from two other points
note: such a point is an arc-midpoint, giving a perpendicular / angle-bisector / antiparallel chord

cue: constant difference of distances to two fixed points
note: the locus is a conic with those foci

cue: constant ratio of distances to two fixed points
note: the locus is an Apollonius circle / fixed line

cue: reflections of a vertex across two cevians through it
note: realize the reflections as images of the feet under a ratio-2 homothety at the vertex
